body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: shim-on-Ground-Loop front-door + Granny-wakes-not-launches
body.intention: I intend that moving the shim onto the Ground Loop changed WHERE the shim lives (persistent front-door) without changing WHETHER a worker reliably receives and works a ticket — verified by observing a real wake->dispatch->work cycle after the change.
body.description:
**What changed:** the worker shim moves from in-process (dies with the device) to a persistent front-door attached to the Ground Loop; Granny stops launching (DS.0 launch_cmd removed) and instead MCP-calls the front-door to wake the device (T-shim-frontdoor-on-groundloop + downstream tickets).

**Predicted unintended effects to watch:**
- Orphaned front-door: front-door up but device won't spawn (bad launch_cmd path moved, env not inherited) -> Granny thinks worker is wakeable but wakes fail -> should trip the stall alarm, NOT silent-drop the ticket.
- Double-listen: if both the old in-process listener AND the front-door receive dispatches during migration, a ticket could be worked twice. Confirm exactly one receiver.
- Availability-flag meaning drift: consumers still reading the flag as 'running' (not 'wakeable') may mis-route. Grep flag readers.
- Number-lease leak: front-door leases a number on wake but never releases on device teardown -> free-list exhausts, 0 (foreground) mis-assigned. Watch instance_name stability across a wake/sleep cycle.

**Signals to watch:** a wake-call to a down worker yields exactly one device-start and one ticket-work; the stall alarm fires when a wake genuinely fails (not spuriously); instance_name stays 'DS.0' across restarts; no ticket worked twice.

**Gate condition:** check by 2026-07-30 (after the front-door + Granny-wake tickets ship and a real wake cycle has run), or when the first non-.0 pool instance is created — whichever first.

**Affected files:** none — observation/verification only.
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket; annotate outcome (occurred/clear/partial) and close.
**Scope boundary:** observe the predicted effects of the front-door migration + wake-not-launch on a live worker; does not cover the identity-surface ticket (that is low-risk S).
